a. Show that, for any interpretation, the sentences $\all{A}{B}$ and
$\somenot{A}{B}$ cannot both be false.

b. Show that, for any interpretation, the sentence $\no{A}{B}$ is true iff the
sentence $\some{A}{B}$ is false.

c. Is there an interpretation on which $\all{A}{B}$ and $\no{A}{B}$ are both
false? Explain your answer.
